meta.name: Japchae (Korean Glass Noodles)
meta.subtitle: Sweet potato noodles stir-fried with vegetables and savory-sweet soy-sesame seasoning.

source.name: ssa.catalogs.cuisines.korean
source.notes: Canonical SSA Japchae: dangmyeon + sesame-soy seasoning, with beef option and fully vegetarian option. Includes make-ahead and weeknight workflow.

summary: Classic japchae: glossy dangmyeon noodles tossed with sautéed vegetables and a soy-sesame-garlic seasoning. Serve warm or room temp.

yield.notes: Makes ~4 generous servings or 6 as a side/banchan.

notes: savory-sweet
toasty sesame
garlic
springy noodles
vegetable-forward

optional.label: Nonstick skillet (for eggs)
optional.notes: Only needed if making optional egg garnish (jidan).

notes: Use gluten-free soy sauce/tamari if needed (check labels).
Egg garnish is optional.

ingredients.label: Sweet potato starch noodles (dangmyeon)
ingredients.notes: Sometimes labeled 'Korean glass noodles' or 'sweet potato noodles'.

ingredients.label: Spinach
ingredients.notes: About 6 oz; can use baby spinach.

ingredients.label: Mushrooms, sliced (shiitake preferred; cremini ok)
ingredients.notes: If using dried shiitake, rehydrate and slice; save soaking liquid for soups.

ingredients.label: Scallions, sliced (white + green parts separated if desired)
Beef (sirloin or ribeye), thinly sliced
ingredients.notes: Optional. For vegetarian, skip beef and optionally add tofu or more mushrooms.

ingredients.label: Firm tofu, pressed and sliced (optional vegetarian protein)
ingredients.notes: If using, pan-sear separately and fold in at the end.

ingredients.label: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: About 4 tbsp; use tamari for gluten-free.

ingredients.label: Toasted sesame oil
ingredients.notes: About 1 tbsp; add more to finish if desired.

ingredients.label: Salt (to taste)
ingredients.notes: Final seasoning depends on soy sauce brand and saltiness.

notes: Cook beef to safe doneness; thin slices cook quickly—avoid overcooking to prevent toughness.
Rinse noodles after boiling to prevent sticking and overcooking; toss with a little oil if holding.

fridge.notes: Japchae keeps well; best re-warmed gently with a splash of water.

notes: Stovetop: add 1–2 tbsp water, cover, warm over medium-low, then uncover to evaporate.
Microwave: cover and heat in short bursts; stir between bursts.
Skip beef; add tofu or extra mushrooms.
Boost umami with a small amount of mushroom powder or a dash of fish-free seaweed broth (optional).
Use pre-julienned carrots + sliced mushrooms; skip egg garnish.
Stir-fry vegetables together (instead of separately) to reduce steps—keep heat high and move fast.
Reduce sugar to 1–2 tsp and add extra sesame + garlic for balance.
Optional: add 1 tsp rice vinegar for brightness.

variations.label: Add bell pepper + scallions
notes: Adds color and aroma; keep slices thin so they soften quickly.

variations.label: Extra sesame-forward
notes: Finish with an additional 1 tsp sesame oil and extra sesame seeds.
Traditional workflow often sautés each vegetable separately to preserve color and texture; SSA version provides both traditional and faster weeknight approaches.
Dangmyeon firms up as it cools—reheat gently with a splash of water to loosen.

steps.title: Mix the seasoning sauce
instructions: In a small bowl, mix soy sauce, sugar, sesame oil, minced garlic, sesame seeds, and a pinch of black pepper.
Set aside. You’ll use part for noodles and part to season vegetables/protein.
checks: Sugar is mostly dissolved; sauce smells nutty-garlicky.

steps.title: Prep vegetables (and optional beef/tofu)
instructions: Slice onion thinly; julienne carrot; slice mushrooms; optionally slice bell pepper and scallions.
If using beef: slice thin and toss with 1–2 tsp of the sauce to season briefly.
If using tofu: press, slice, and plan to sear it separately.
checks: Vegetables are uniformly cut for even cooking.

steps.title: Blanch spinach
instructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Blanch spinach 20–30 seconds until wilted bright green.
Drain and rinse under cold water. Squeeze out excess water.
Season lightly with a tiny pinch of salt and a few drops of sesame oil (optional).
checks: Spinach is bright green, not dull/overcooked; well-squeezed to avoid watery japchae.

steps.title: Cook dangmyeon noodles
instructions: In boiling water, cook dangmyeon per package (typically 6–8 minutes) until translucent and chewy.
Drain and rinse briefly with cool water to stop cooking.
Shake off water well, then toss noodles with 1–2 tsp of the sauce to prevent sticking.
Optional: snip noodles with kitchen shears into shorter lengths for easier eating.
checks: Noodles are translucent, springy, not mushy; lightly coated so they don’t clump.

steps.title: Stir-fry vegetables (and optional beef/tofu)
instructions: Heat a large skillet/wok over medium-high. Add a small splash of neutral oil.
Stir-fry onion 2–3 minutes until softened; remove to bowl.
Stir-fry carrot 1–2 minutes; remove.
Stir-fry mushrooms 2–3 minutes until softened and lightly browned; remove.
Optional beef: quickly stir-fry 1–2 minutes until just cooked; remove.
Optional tofu: sear slices until golden; remove.
Weeknight shortcut: stir-fry all vegetables together 4–6 minutes (high heat, keep moving).
checks: Vegetables are tender-crisp (not soggy). Beef (if used) is not overcooked.

steps.title: Combine everything and toss
instructions: Add noodles to the skillet (or a big mixing bowl). Add cooked vegetables, spinach, and optional beef/tofu.
Add remaining sauce gradually, tossing well. Taste and adjust: more soy for salt, a pinch more sugar for balance, or a touch more sesame oil for aroma.
Warm through 1–2 minutes if needed, stirring gently to avoid tearing noodles.
checks: Noodles are evenly seasoned and glossy; vegetables are distributed; taste is balanced savory-sweet.

steps.title: Garnish and serve
instructions: Optional egg garnish (jidan): beat egg, cook a thin omelet, slice into thin strips, and top the japchae.
Top with extra sesame seeds and scallions if desired.
Serve warm or at room temperature.
checks: Final dish is glossy, fragrant with sesame, and not watery.
